postmanEvidence: Postman Desktop 11.71.7 and newman@6.2.2 runtime source both expose a 180000 ms global timeout default with request/script timeouts set to Infinity.
Newman probes record busy-loop, timer-drain, uncleared interval, and console/output behavior below PostMeter policy ceilings.

commands: npm install postman-runtime@7.50.0 postman-sandbox@6.2.2 in an isolated temporary directory and inspect lib/runner/index.js, lib/runner/extensions/event.command.js, and lib/sandbox/execute.js
npm install postman-runtime@7.39.1 postman-sandbox@4.7.1 in an isolated temporary directory and inspect the same runtime/sandbox files for newman@6.2.2
Run bounded newman@6.2.2 probes for busy-loop duration, async timer drain, uncleared intervals, and console/log behavior
Run test/fixtures/postman/differential-runtime-limits.collection.json through PostMeter and optional live Newman comparison

runtimeSourceDefaults.postmanSandbox622: Only enforces script timeout when the runtime passes a finite options.timeout; otherwise timers drain until empty.
runtimeSourceDefaults.postmanSandbox471: Only enforces script timeout when the runtime passes a finite options.timeout; otherwise timers drain until empty.

newmanEmpiricalProbes.unclearedInterval: did not complete before an external 8s process timeout; runtime global timeout is the documented/source-level safety net
newmanEmpiricalProbes.consoleLogCount150: passed; newman JSON reporter did not expose console events as hard-comparable output

postmanSourceCaps.legacyResponseSize: postman-sandbox legacy globals use MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE = 50MiB for old responseBody-style globals
postmanSourceCaps.visualizer: pm.visualizer.set records template/data/options without an explicit source-level size cap
postmanSourceCaps.console: postman-sandbox forwards console events; no stable source-level output count/line cap was found
postmanSourceCaps.packages: postman-sandbox package loading does not expose a stable source-level package source/count cap equivalent to PostMeter reviewed-cache policy

matchedOrRelaxed: Default script execution timeout increased to 180000ms to match the audited Postman Runtime default global timeout safety net while keeping a deterministic per-script worker kill.
Default HTTP request timeout and pm.sendRequest timeout ceiling increased to 180000ms so normal brokered script requests share the same audited default budget.
Async timer drain and cleared interval behavior are fixture-covered through differential-runtime-limits.collection.json.
productionResourcePolicy: PostMeter keeps explicit caps for worker heap, broker payload bytes, pm.sendRequest response body bytes, final worker result bytes, console entry count/line length, visualizer template/data/output/assets, reviewed package count/source/dependency depth, mock state, and workspace payload sizes.

These caps are required by the production sandbox contract because scripts must not be able to exhaust parent IPC, renderer, storage, or worker memory. Postman/Newman source either has no stable equivalent cap for these channels or relies on host/runtime process limits.

The full Postman import claim treats these caps as documented local resource policy rather than unsupported Postman script APIs. Normal API behavior below the ceilings is differential-covered, while host-exhaustion-sized payloads remain adversarial/security cases.

notes: This audit completes and clears runtime.resource-limit-parity: PostMeter matches the observed timeout/timer behavior, keeps documented production resource ceilings for channels where Postman/Newman rely on host process limits, and verifies the oversized cases through adversarial tests rather than treating them as unsupported Postman APIs.
